title: Schoenorchis aurea
intro: Schoenorchis aurea, also known as the golden orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Schoenorchis aurea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Schoenorchis aurea is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Schoenorchis aurea is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Schoenorchis and the family Orchidaceae. The genus Schoenorchis is a group of terrestrial orchids that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Within the Orchidaceae family, Schoenorchis aurea is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and its evolutionary relationships with other orchids.
